pi_items.source: Mathematical verification from study data (TP=180, FN=20): Sensitivity = 180/(180+20) = 0.90 = 90.0%
Mathematical verification from study data (TN=760, FP=40): Specificity = 760/(760+40) = 0.95 = 95.0%
Mathematical verification: PPV = TP/(TP+FP) = 180/220 = 0.8182 = 81.82%, rounded to 81.8% within tolerance of 0.2%
Mathematical verification: NPV = TN/(TN+FN) = 760/780 = 0.9744 = 97.44%, rounded to 97.4% within tolerance of 0.2%

Mathematical verification using Bayes' theorem: LR+ = 90%/(1-95%) = 18; Pre-test odds = 0.02/0.98 = 0.0204; Post-test odds = 0.0204*18 = 0.3673; PPV = 0.3673/(1+0.3673) = 26.87%, within tolerance of 0.2%

pi_items.metadata_value: Epi Info 7 StatCalc contains Diagnostic Test calculator
pi_items.source: CDC Epi Info 7 documentation lists StatCalc modules as: Sample Size & Power, Chi Square for Trend, Tables (2x2, 2xn), Poisson, Binomial, Matched Pair Case Control. No 'Diagnostic Test' calculator found in official Epi Info 7 StatCalc. OpenEpi.com has a separate Diagnostic Test calculator, but this is not part of Epi Info 7 StatCalc. https://www.cdc.gov/epiinfo/user-guide/statcalc/statcalcintro.html
Path cannot be verified without accessing Windows container environment. This is a Windows-specific path in a Docker container.
Tolerance value is mathematically appropriate for the calculated percentages, allowing for rounding differences in PPV (81.82 vs 81.8), NPV (97.44 vs 97.4), and Airport PPV (26.87 vs 26.9)

privileged_info_summary: The task evaluates diagnostic test accuracy using a synthetic hypothetical scenario involving a Rapid Fever Test. The study data consists of 1,000 patients (200 disease-positive, 800 disease-negative) with test results: 180 true positives and 40 false positives, yielding 20 false negatives and 760 true negatives. Mathematical verification confirms all expected diagnostic metrics are correct: Sensitivity=90.0%, Specificity=95.0%, Baseline PPV=81.82% (claimed 81.8%), Baseline NPV=97.44% (claimed 97.4%), and Airport PPV at 2% prevalence=26.87% (claimed 26.9%). All calculated values fall within the specified tolerance of 0.2%. However, the task description claims to use an 'Epi Info 7 StatCalc Diagnostic Test calculator' that does not appear to exist in the official Epi Info 7 StatCalc module; the actual StatCalc tools available are Sample Size & Power, Chi Square for Trend, Tables (2x2, 2xn), Poisson, Binomial, and Matched Pair Case Control.
